name: Proto-West-Semitic
notes: Proto-West-Semitic (~3500–3000 BCE). Excludes Akkadian and Eblaite (East Semitic). Ancestor of all surviving Semitic languages. Consonant inventory nearly identical to Proto-Semitic; differences are primarily morphological and accentual. *ś /ɬ/ lateral fricative still distinct. Spirantisation of stops (bgdkpt pattern) is a LATER innovation (Canaanite/Hebrew) and NOT a Proto-West-Semitic feature.

sources.author: Lipiński, E.
sources.title: Semitic Languages: Outline of a Comparative Grammar
